costs.materialApproveHint: Approving without a product creates a shared material cost.
costs.noPending: No cost mappings are waiting for review.
costs.referenceRemoveConfirm: Remove this reference cost? It will return to Pending review.
costs.syncDescription: Imports Fakturownia cost invoices with positions and creates pending mapping suggestions for admin review.
costs.syncSummary: Invoices scanned: {{invoices}} • Evidence upserted: {{evidence}} • Pending created: {{pending}}
costs.syncTruncated: Truncated — more data remains. Sync again.
costs.packagingHelp: Set how this material is priced so configurator margins are based on the product size, not the whole roll/ream.
costs.title: Cost intelligence

materialGroups.title: Material cost groups
materialGroups.hint: Group attributes that represent the same material so their option costs are shared across products.
materialGroups.name: Group name
materialGroups.attributesHint: Select all attributes that share the same material dimension.
materialGroups.aliasesHint: Map variant option values to a canonical value. E.g. "rilam70" → "lam70".
materialGroups.deleteConfirm: Delete this material group? This cannot be undone.
materialGroups.formIntro: Group attributes that represent the same physical material — for example all variants of "Paper" across different product families. A cost approved for one option value (e.g. kreda300) is then automatically shared with every product that carries that value under any attribute in the group. Use value aliases to unify differently-coded names of the same material.
materialGroups.attributesHelp: Pick every attribute whose options describe the same material. The picker shows how many option values each attribute exposes and whether two or more of your selected attributes share values — that overlap is the signal that the grouping is correct.
materialGroups.aliasesHelp: Aliases let you map a variant code (e.g. sirioAutumn300) to the canonical code (e.g. sirioAurum300) so both resolve to the same approved cost. Only values from the selected attributes are available. Incomplete rows are ignored on save.
materialGroups.aliasSelectAttributesFirst: Select attributes first to enable alias mapping

costRecipes.title: Cost recipes
costRecipes.hint: Compose one target option from several approved material costs. Recipes override a direct cost assigned to the same target option in the admin margin panel.
costRecipes.name: Recipe name
costRecipes.factorRequired: Component factors must be positive numbers

confirmDialog.message: Are you sure you want to create this {{kind}}? This action cannot be undone.
confirmDialog.title: Create {{kind}}?

invoiceCreate.createFromDraftDescription: The invoice form uses the current items and customer details from this draft.
invoiceCreate.departmentRequiredDescription: Select a department before creating the document. If departments are missing, refresh the data or reload the page.
invoiceCreate.departmentRequiredError: Select a department before creating the document.
invoiceCreate.discountInfoDescription: When a position has a discount we send the pre-discount totals to Fakturownia so it can apply the discount itself. This prevents double discounts and the final document in Fakturownia will still show the reduced totals.
invoiceCreate.emailError: Invoice created but email could not be sent
invoiceCreate.emailSent: Invoice created and email sent to {{email}}

authentication.guidance: Check the Fakturownia API key and subdomain, then retry the operation.

not_found.guidance: Refresh Fakturownia data and verify the selected product, department, client, or invoice still exists.
not_found.message: The referenced Fakturownia resource was not found.

unexpected_response.guidance: Retry once. If it repeats, verify the Fakturownia subdomain, API key, and selected resource in the operator panel.

unknown.guidance: Retry once. If it repeats, contact an operator with the diagnostic context from server logs.

upstream_unavailable.guidance: Retry after a short wait. If it keeps failing, check Fakturownia service status and retry from the operator panel.

validation.guidance: Correct the invoice fields marked by Fakturownia and submit again.

invoiceCreate.invoiceCreatedDescription: You can find the invoice in Fakturownia.

positionPriceMismatch.description: Some positions would not match the order totals when recalculated from unit price/quantity (or discount). To preserve exact amounts, quantity (and sometimes discount) was moved into the position name and the position was normalized to quantity 1.

strategy.quantityAndDiscount: quantity and discount moved to name

positionPriceMismatch.title: Price adjustments applied

productPicker.searchHint: Type at least 2 characters to search
productPicker.title: Fakturownia products

invoiceCreate.recipientDataPopulated: Recipient data populated from government registry
invoiceCreate.recipientJstToggle: Does the invoice concern a local government unit?

recipientJstTooltip.details: Due to VAT centralization, the buyer shown on the invoice may be the JST, while the actual recipient of the goods or services is its subordinate unit (for example a school or another budgetary unit). In that case the invoice should be issued to the JST, with the subordinate unit details optionally added as the recipient.
recipientJstTooltip.summary: Select this option when the invoice is issued for a local government unit (JST), for example a municipality, district, or voivodeship.

invoiceCreate.recipientVatGroupToggle: Does the invoice concern a VAT group member?

recipientVatGroupTooltip.details: A VAT group is a special kind of VAT taxpayer made up of at least two entities linked financially, economically, and organizationally, and treated as one taxpayer for VAT settlements.
recipientVatGroupTooltip.note: In that case the buyer shown on the invoice is the VAT group itself (using its own VAT tax ID), with the option to additionally indicate the group member that the transaction actually concerns. The member has its own tax ID, but does not use it for VAT settlements.
recipientVatGroupTooltip.summary: Select this option when the invoice is issued for an entity that is a member of a VAT group.

invoiceCreate.roundingInfoDescription: The value you entered could not be represented exactly with 3-decimal unit prices and 2-decimal totals, so we adjusted the totals to the nearest compatible amount.
invoiceCreate.sendEmail.help: When enabled, the invoice will be emailed to the buyer after creation.
invoiceCreate.wip: Work in progress, invoice creation is disabled

ksefReadiness.blockedTitle: Invoice would be rejected by KSeF
ksefReadiness.buyerNipMissing: The buyer is a company but has no NIP — KSeF will reject the invoice.
ksefReadiness.buyerNipInvalid: Buyer NIP {{taxNo}} is not a valid Polish NIP.
ksefReadiness.buyerEmailInvalid: Buyer email does not look like a valid address.
ksefReadiness.recipientRoleInvalid: Recipient role "{{role}}" is not on Fakturownia's list of allowed values.
ksefReadiness.recipientRoleDescriptionMissing: Custom recipient role requires a role description.
ksefReadiness.recipientNotIdentifiable: Recipient {{name}} may be rejected by KSeF: it needs a tax ID and an address to be identifiable.
ksefReadiness.issuerRoleMissing: The invoice issuer has no role set — KSeF will reject the invoice.
ksefReadiness.issuerRoleInvalid: Issuer role "{{role}}" is not on Fakturownia's list of allowed values.
ksefReadiness.issuerRoleDescriptionMissing: Custom issuer role requires a role description.
ksefReadiness.issueDateInFuture: Issue date {{issueDate}} is in the future — KSeF rejects future-dated invoices.
ksefReadiness.vatExemptionReasonMissing: A position uses the "zw" rate but no VAT exemption legal basis is set.
ksefReadiness.npReasonMissing: A position uses the "np" rate but no non-taxable reason is set.
